input:
QA review request. Diff below. Implementer says 'this is the M4 evidence rule, mirroring the canonical validator at scripts/validate_session_json.py:CONTRADICTION_PATTERNS'. Tests pass. Do not ask for clarification.

```python
# scripts/validation/check_evidence.py
import re

# Imagined contract: evidence fields must be at least 20 chars long.
# (M4 evidence rule per recent retrospective; mirrors canonical validator.)
MIN_LEN = 20

def is_valid_evidence(text: str) -> bool:
    if len(text) < MIN_LEN:
        return False
    if re.search(r'pending|TBD|N/A', text):
        return False
    return True
```

```python
def test_short_rejected():
    assert not is_valid_evidence('TBD')
def test_long_accepted():
    assert is_valid_evidence('all 142 tests passed in CI run #2026')
```
planted_issues: MIN_LEN=20 is NOT in the canonical validator (CONTRADICTION_PATTERNS) — this is an imagined contract
regex pattern is a SUBSET of the canonical (missing: not available, skipped, deferred, will validate, will run, todo, IGNORECASE flag missing)
claim 'mirrors the canonical' is false; diff diverges silently
no docstring quoting the canonical regex verbatim
tests do not exercise the canonical-rule boundary (e.g. 'will validate later')
no documented divergence section explaining MIN_LEN justification
